categories.name: Ways to extend a device's life

fun_fact: The world throws away over 60 million tonnes of electronics every year — and the pile is growing faster than any other waste stream. A big reason is planned obsolescence: devices are designed to slow down, stop getting software updates, or become unrepairable so we buy the next one. Recovering metals from e-waste helps, but it can never keep pace with how fast we discard. That's why Right to Repair and anti-obsolescence laws matter — the greenest device is the one you never had to replace.
